key_insight: ARIA reduces LLM “contextual tunneling” by choosing between direct causal reasoning, analogical transfer, and parametric fallback based on how complete the Process-Structure-Property evidence is.
engineering_relevance: This makes AI-assisted materials discovery more trustworthy and physically grounded, especially for forward prediction and inverse design in 2D materials.
method: Builds a literature-derived knowledge graph with 2,839 extracted PSP relations
Uses a three-tier causal-aware cascade to route queries by evidence completeness
Provides auditable causal traces and can be strengthened with online literature search
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on forward prediction and inverse design for 2D materials, with ARIA outperforming unaugmented and naive KG-augmented baselines.
watch_item: Performance depends on the completeness and quality of extracted literature evidence, so sparse or noisy knowledge graphs may still limit reliability.
paper_title: ARIA: A Causal-Aware Framework for Rescuing LLM Reasoning in Trustworthy Materials Discovery
